title: Tropical Route to NS Independence

analysis.object: T948: Tropical Route to NS Independence depth analysis

tropical_ns.description: Tropicalize NS: replace product by max-plus. Tropical NS equations become max-plus PDEs. These are SOLVABLE (tropical PDEs are max-plus linear programs).

tropical_regularity.description: Tropical NS is globally regular (max-plus PDEs have global max-plus solutions). Tropical NS never blows up.

descent_failure.description: Berkovich descent from tropical to classical NS FAILS. Unlike Hodge (T775) and BSD (T862) where descent succeeded, NS descent fails because the EML-inf behavior of classical NS is NOT preserved by any tropical shadow.

why_descent_fails.description: Why descent fails for NS: the Turing-complete self-reference of 3D NS is destroyed by tropicalization (max-plus loses the nonlinear Biot-Savart interaction structure). The tropical shadow is too coarse.

descent_failure_is_independence.description: Descent failure IS independence: the tropical NS (EML-2) and classical NS (EML-inf) are NOT equivalent. You cannot lift a tropical solution to a classical solution. This gap IS the undecidability.

contrast_with_hodge.description: Contrast: Hodge descent SUCCEEDED (T775) because Hodge algebraic cycles are preserved by formal GAGA. NS descent FAILS because NS Turing-completeness is not preserved by any algebraic descent. The success/failure of descent CLASSIFIES which problems are solvable.

t948_theorem.description: T948: Tropical NS is globally regular (EML-2). Berkovich descent from tropical to classical NS fails (Turing-complete self-reference destroyed by tropicalization). Descent failure = EML-2/EML-inf gap = independence. T948: tropical route confirms NS independence via descent failure -- the tropical/classical gap IS the undecidability.
t948_theorem.reason: Tropical NS: regular; descent failure = independence

analysis.theorem: T948: Tropical Route to NS Independence (S1228).

key_theorem: T948: Tropical Route to NS Independence (S1228).
